text.title: Impl Plan Components Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Impl Plan Components. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Every TASK references exactly one primary component; cross-component changes split into multiple TASKs.
rule.rationale: Multi-component tasks block parallel execution and hide dependency edges.

rule.statement: Components are named by stable identifier (file path, module name, service name); ad-hoc labels rejected.
rule.rationale: Ad-hoc labels prevent dependency graphs from matching the codebase.

rule.statement: Every TASK lists its upstream-component dependencies; implicit dependencies rejected.
rule.rationale: Implicit dependencies cause race conditions in parallel execution.

rule.statement: Component dependency graph is acyclic; cycles indicate a layering bug to be resolved before execution.
rule.rationale: Cycles cannot be executed in order; the impl-plan must be restructured.

rule.statement: Each component lists at least one owner; orphan components rejected.
rule.rationale: Orphan components rot; explicit ownership forces accountability.

rule.statement: Final artefact includes the component dependency graph as adjacency list; downstream tools consume it.
rule.rationale: Without a machine-readable graph, parallel schedulers cannot plan.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-impl-plan-components.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
